headline: Carnival Pops 10%, Norwegian Rises 7%: Six Days After Falling on Fuel Fears, Cruise Stocks Are Surging
summary: Six days ago, cruise stocks were sinking. On April 2, shares of Carnival (NYSE:CCL) and Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) stock fell roughly 4% on fuel cost fears as WTI crude oil surged from around $80 per barrel in early March to above $113 per barrel by late March, squeezing the outlook for two of the most ... headline: A Look At Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) Valuation After New Board Appointments And Norwegian Luna Christening
summary: New board appointments and a fleet addition put Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) in focus Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) has drawn investor attention after appointing several high profile directors, including former leaders from British Airways and Disney, as well as christening its newest ship, Norwegian Luna, in Miami. These governance and fleet developments arrive with the shares at US$18.73, after a 3.3% one day decline and a 6.6% drop over the past month. Over the past 3...

headline: Delta Stock Soars After Earnings. These Airlines Are Climbing Even Higher.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging 8% on Wednesday.  United Airlines  rose 13%,  Southwest Airlines  jumped 11%, and  Norwegian Cruise Line  was rising 12%.  Delta’s guidance may be a reason it’s lagging behind slightly—if an 8% rise can described in such terms.

headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.
